title: DARPA-developed autonomous helicopter technology transitions to U.S. Army
raw_text: An experimental, fly-by-wire H-60Mx Black Hawk, equipped with DARPA-funded autonomy, has been delivered to the U.S. Army for testing.

title: Translate your bio-attribution research into national security impact
raw_text: Register for the DARPA Bio-Attribution Challenge to win a share of $180,000 in prizes.

title: Quantum Benchmarking Initiative expands quest to separate hype from reality
raw_text: New QBI solicitation invites new entrants and approaches.

title: DARPA’s new X-76: the speed of a jet, the freedom of a helicopter
raw_text: SPRINT’s experimental aircraft is now being built by Bell Textron, Inc. following a successful Critical Design Review.

title: Upgrading biomass waste into strategic materials
raw_text: The Fleetwood program aims to produce critical chemicals from widely available domestic resources.

title: ROCkN enables GPS-free operations
raw_text: The Robust Optical Clock Network (ROCkN) program enables precision timekeeping, even in contested and/or GPS-denied environments.

title: Turning proteins into PROSE
raw_text: The PROtein SEquencing (PROSE) program launches to build microsystems that directly read and decipher complex proteins.

title: National Museum of the U.S. Air Force to host DARPA Lift Challenge
raw_text: The outdoor competition, including the final flyoff and awards ceremony on Aug. 9, will be open to the public.

title: Not such a long shot
raw_text: The X‑68A completed a series of technical milestones, moving its air-launched uninhabited vehicle closer to flight test.

title: Thermonat makes nanoscale thermal prediction practical for real-world chip design
raw_text: Rapid-start research moves from exploration to commercialization.

title: STADLER reshapes knowledge work at a 230-year-old company
raw_text: Learn how STADLER uses ChatGPT to transform knowledge work, saving time and accelerating productivity across 650 employees.

title: Inside our approach to the Model Spec
raw_text: Learn how OpenAI’s Model Spec serves as a public framework for model behavior, balancing safety, user freedom, and accountability as AI systems advance.

title: Introducing the OpenAI Safety Bug Bounty program
raw_text: OpenAI launches a Safety Bug Bounty program to identify AI abuse and safety risks, including agentic vulnerabilities, prompt injection, and data exfiltration.

title: Helping developers build safer AI experiences for teens
raw_text: OpenAI releases prompt-based teen safety policies for developers using gpt-oss-safeguard, helping moderate age-specific risks in AI systems.

title: Powering product discovery in ChatGPT
raw_text: ChatGPT introduces richer, visually immersive shopping powered by the Agentic Commerce Protocol, enabling product discovery, side-by-side comparisons, and merchant integration.

title: Update on the OpenAI Foundation
raw_text: The OpenAI Foundation announces plans to invest at least $1 billion in curing diseases, economic opportunity, AI resilience, and community programs.

title: Creating with Sora Safely
raw_text: To address the novel safety challenges posed by a state-of-the-art video model as well as a new social creation platform, we’ve built Sora 2 and the Sora app with safety at the foundation. Our approach is anchored in concrete protections.

title: How we monitor internal coding agents for misalignment
raw_text: How OpenAI uses chain-of-thought monitoring to study misalignment in internal coding agents—analyzing real-world deployments to detect risks and strengthen AI safety safeguards.

title: OpenAI to acquire Astral
raw_text: Accelerates Codex growth to power the next generation of Python developer tools

title: OpenAI Japan announces Japan Teen Safety Blueprint to put teen safety first
raw_text: OpenAI Japan announces the Japan Teen Safety Blueprint, introducing stronger age protections, parental controls, and well-being safeguards for teens using generative AI.

title: Introducing GPT-5.4 mini and nano
raw_text: GPT-5.4 mini and nano are smaller, faster versions of GPT-5.4 optimized for coding, tool use, multimodal reasoning, and high-volume API and sub-agent workloads.

title: Equipping workers with insights about compensation
raw_text: New research shows Americans send nearly 3 million daily messages to ChatGPT asking about compensation and earnings, helping close the wage information gap.
